title: Joplin Server
description: Self-hosted sync server for the Joplin open-source note-taking and to-do app, with end-to-end-encrypted multi-device sync and sharing.
installation: Deploy with the bundled PostgreSQL add-on; rotate the Postgres password (and after first login the admin password) from the template defaults before production. Joplin Server validates incoming requests against APP_BASE_URL, so it is set to the deployed public URL via {{KUBERO_APP_URL}} — if you change the hostname you must update this or clients get an 'invalid origin' error. The server listens on port 22300 (APP_PORT) and serves both the web admin UI and the sync API. Storage driver defaults to the database (DB_CLIENT=pg), so notes, attachments and blobs live in Postgres; the joplin-storage volume mounted at /home/joplin/storage is only used if you switch to the filesystem storage driver. On first run the database is migrated automatically; log in to the web UI with the default admin account admin@localhost / admin and change the password immediately, then point Joplin desktop/mobile clients at the URL using the 'Joplin Server' sync target. No external object store, search engine or SMTP is required for a base install (configure MAILER_* env vars later if you want email invitations).
